calm.name: Stress Relief
calm.subtitle: Classic 4–6 breathing pattern
calm.description: Sometimes tension takes over, and we need a pause just to catch our breath. Think of this practice as your personal reset button. It gently calms the nervous system, helps release anxiety, and brings the mind back to a clearer, steadier place.
calm.fullDescription: The 4–6 breathing pattern uses ten-second cycles to help relieve stress quickly. A smooth 4-second inhale flows straight into a 6-second exhale with a deep, steady rhythm. This longer exhale helps stimulate the vagus nerve, slow the heart rate, and reduce cortisol.
calm.final_text: You’ve given yourself a precious minute of quiet. Let this gentle sense of calm and ease stay with you for as long as you can.

square.name: Focus
square.subtitle: Box breathing: 4–4–4–4
square.description: Box breathing can be a steady anchor when stress rises or things feel out of control. The even rhythm of inhales, exhales, and gentle pauses creates a sense of stability. Simply follow the prompts: with each cycle, tension softens, the body feels more grounded, and the mind becomes clearer.
square.fullDescription: The symmetrical 4–4–4–4 pattern helps stabilize the nervous system and improve concentration. Each cycle is made up of four equal 4-second phases: inhale, hold, exhale, and pause. The breath holds help regulate carbon dioxide levels and slow the heart rate, while the steady rhythm supports focus and helps restore balance.
square.final_text: You’ve returned to balance and focus. Take a deep breath, let a small smile appear, and step back into your day with steadiness.

deep_relax.name: Deep Relaxation
deep_relax.subtitle: 4–2–6 breathing pattern
deep_relax.description: When the day has been long and tension has built up in the body, this practice helps you gently settle and come back to yourself. A brief pause after the inhale gives the mind a moment to quiet down, while the longer exhale brings a deeper sense of release. It’s an ideal choice for easing muscle fatigue and winding down in the evening.
deep_relax.fullDescription: The 4–2–6 pattern uses 12-second cycles for deep relaxation. A smooth 4-second inhale leads into a brief 2-second pause at the top of the breath. The cycle ends with a long, gentle 6-second descent on the exhale. This structure can help release muscle tension and shift the nervous system into rest mode.
deep_relax.final_text: The tension has eased, making room for softness and calm. Stay with this feeling a little longer, and allow yourself the rest you deserve.
